text: Yehyemeeyɛle abo zo amra ne dɛnlanle Havelaa nee Hyulu mɔɔ saa ɛlɛkɔ Aselea a ɛnee ɔwɔ Yigyibiti aduduleɛ la. Bɛ nee Ebileham abo zo amra mɔɔ hale la andɛnla. Yisɔ nee Gyekɔbo awolɛ ne
translated: The Egyptians mistreated the Israelites and made them suffer under harsh conditions. They forced them to build the cities of Pithom and Rameses as storage centers for King Pharaoh. But the more the Egyptians oppressed them, the more the Israelites multiplied and spread.

ref: “Let Rebekah stay with us for a week or ten days,” they answered. “Then she may go.”
text: Relɛbɛka adiema nrenya ne nee ɔ nli zele ye kɛ, “Maa kakula raalɛ ne nee yɛ ɛdɛnla kenle kɛyɛ bulu na ɛ nee ye ahɔ.”
translated: Rabbit told his grandmother that he said, "I will go and tell my mother that she should come and prepare soup for us to eat."

ref: Nahor also had another wife. Her name was Reumah, and she had four sons: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
text: Ye mgbɔnla Wulumaa ɔdaye noko ɔwole Tiba, Geeham, Teehahye, yɛɛ Meeyaka.
translated: On the first day of the week, which is Sunday, there is Tiba, Geeham, Teehahye, and Meeyaka.

ref: Finally, the king sent for Abram and said to him, “What have you done to me? Why didn't you tell me Sarai was your wife?
text: Yemɔti belemgbunli ne vɛlɛle Ebilam na ɔbizale ye kɛ, “Duzu debie a wɔyɛ la? Duzu ati a wɔanga wɔangile me kɛ ɔle ɛ ye ɛ?
translated: Yemɔti went and met Ebilam and asked him, "What is this that you have done? What is the tree that you have not cut for me that you are doing this?"

ref: I pray God will give Japheth more and more land and let him take over the territory of Shem. May Canaan be his slave.”
text: Nyamenle ɛmaa Gyeefɛte abo zo ɛdɛlɛ! Ɔ bo zo amra nee Hyɛm abo zo amra ɛdɛnla! Keena bayɛ Gyeefɛte kanranli.”
translated: The animal that Gyeefɛte killed is very big! He killed it and gave it to Hyɛm to kill another one! Today, we will see Gyeefɛte's strength.
